questions.question: How long do veneers last?
questions.answer: Porcelain veneers typically last 15-20 years with proper care, while composite veneers last 5-7 years.

questions.question: Is the procedure painful?
questions.answer: No, the procedure is performed under local anesthesia and is virtually painless. Any mild discomfort after treatment can be managed with over-the-counter pain medication.

questions.question: Are veneers permanent?
questions.answer: Yes, veneers are a permanent treatment as a small amount of enamel must be removed. They will need to be replaced eventually, but with proper care, they can last many years.

questions.question: Can veneers stain?
questions.answer: Porcelain veneers are highly resistant to staining. However, the bonding material can slightly discolor over time, so good oral hygiene and regular dental visits are important.

care.title: Caring for Your Veneers
care.description: Maintain your investment with proper care
instructions: Brush twice daily with non-abrasive toothpaste
Avoid biting hard objects or foods
Wear a night guard if you grind your teeth
